impact:
SYMPTOM (solid, reproduced two ways). V2's error is monotonic in BLEND while the wet path is correct:
  HF(5-13k) mean:  BL=1.00 +0.3dB (good) | BL=0.95 +8.7 | BL=0.90 +11.2 | BL=0.50 +15.3
  ab_report FR@:   BL=0.90 -> 8000:+13.2  12000:+27.1     BL=0.95 -> 8000:+10.4  12000:+24.4
  BL=0.50 top octave: 12.9kHz plugin -8.9dB vs pedal -63.3dB (delta +54.4); 16.3kHz delta +58.3.
NOT a noise-floor artifact — the pedal has real, deep HF rolloff there; the plugin passes the dry tap with only the tone stack's 7.2kHz R35||C32 pole.

*** ROOT CAUSE IS OPEN. MY FIRST HYPOTHESIS WAS WRONG — DO NOT RE-TRY IT. ***
I originally blamed kOutputMakeup[2]=0.123 (commented "still the placeholder") feeding kDryGain[2]=10.569, guessing the dry tap was ~12dB hot. REFUTED by ab_report's LEVEL/gain column: every V2 capture gain-matches to ~0dB (BL=1.00: -0.2/+1.4/+0.1; BL=0.95 -1.1; BL=0.90 -0.9; BL=0.50 -0.1). The broadband LEVEL is already CORRECT at every blend, so kOutputMakeup[2]/kDryGain[2] are not 12dB wrong.

Note also: kOutputMakeup CANNOT be fitted from gain-matched data at all — it is a global output scalar, exactly what the analysis gain-match removes. It is only visible in the raw LEVEL column (and only trustworthy for the identically-staged V1E+V2 set). Likewise OfflineRender's --out-makeup scales outGain ONLY; kDryGain is a hardcoded constant it does not move, so --out-makeup cannot probe the dry/wet ratio.

WHAT THE EVIDENCE NOW SAYS: level right + HF badly wrong = a FREQUENCY-DEPENDENT dry-path error, not a gain constant. Candidates, untested:
  (a) The real V2 dry path has HF rolloff we have not modelled (netlists.md V1 reads "IN_B -> direct (no cap) -> BLEND VR50.a" — full bandwidth; that read may be wrong or incomplete).
  (b) Dry/wet PHASE cancellation at HF that the model gets wrong. Suggestive: the pedal at BL=0.50 (-63.3dB @12.9k) is 6.7dB QUIETER than half its own BL=1.00 wet alone (-50.6 -6 = -56.6), which a pure crossfade cannot do — that is destructive interference, so the pedal's dry is NOT simply absent up there.
  (c) BLEND pot impedance loading modelled as too ideal a crossfade (cf. CLAUDE.md: real blend leaks the off-side, cap-impedance-limited, frequency-dependent, ~-22..-56dB asymmetric; and ISS-006's "NodalCircuit impedance loading" note on V1L).
  (d) NAM capture fidelity at HF (least likely — the wet path's HF captures fine at BL=1.00).

CASCADE (unchanged, still holds): blocks honest diagnosis of ISS-003 (V2 12.5k/16k, OPPOSITE sign so the excess MASKS it at BL<1.00) and ISS-005 (BASS hump; -8.4dB low-mid excess at BL=0.50). V1L does NOT show this, so it is V2-specific.

*** NO NEW CAPTURE-BASED ISOLATION IS POSSIBLE (confirmed 2026-07-16) ***
There is no BLEND=0 capture, and confirmed by checking every capture's full settings dict: no two captures in the 12-capture matrix share ALL non-blend knob settings, so there is also no algebraic way to isolate the dry path by differencing two existing captures. Additional untrusted captures exist but the user has flagged their values as unreliable AND they don't cover the missing knob-isolation combinations either. Do not propose "capture a BLEND=0 file" or "diff two same-setting captures" as next steps — neither is available. Resolve via (a)/(c) first — re-derive the BLEND network's true frequency-dependent transfer analytically/from circuit.md's documented cap-impedance-limited isolation figures — before any capture-based confirmation is possible.

Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section C); python3.11 analysis/ab_report.py --filter V2 --os 8.
title: V2 dry path passes far too much HF at BLEND<1.00 (+27 dB @12k) — level is correct, SHAPE is not

phase1_kOutputMakeup.note: kOutputMakeup[2]=0.123 is already calibrated within 0.4 dB. ISS-008 is NOT a level/gain issue.

phase2_A1_spice_cross_check.result: SPICE TARGETS MET — MAGNITUDE CORRECT, CANDIDATE SHIFTED TO (a)
phase2_A1_spice_cross_check.key_finding: At BL=0.50 / 12.9 kHz, plugin wet ≈ −75 dBr at blend wiper while dry ≈ −8 dBr. Dry dominates by 67 dB — phase cancellation CANNOT explain the 54 dB discrepancy at these amplitude ratios. The pedal's dry MUST be similarly attenuated (~63 dB down) at 12.9 kHz, requiring ~55 dB of unmodelled dry-path HF rolloff.

phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.result: V1L SHOWS SAME MECHANISM — CONFIRMS FLAT DRY IS THE ROOT CAUSE
phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.v1l_swing: +35.4 dB from wet to partial blend
phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.v2_swing: +44.0 dB from wet to partial blend
phase2_B1_v1l_blend_comparison.key_finding: Both V1L and V2 show large positive FR error at HF when BL<1.00 (flat dry mixed in). V1L starts with a too-quiet wet (−28.5 dB) so adding dry helps. V2 starts with a correct wet (−2.8 dB) so adding dry destroys the match. The mechanism is identical: the plugin's dry path is flat (full bandwidth), the pedal's is not.

diagnostics.root_cause: CANDIDATE (a) — unmodelled dry-path HF rolloff. The pedal's real dry path has significant HF attenuation (~55 dB at 12.9 kHz) that the netlist's 'IN_B → direct (no cap) → BLEND VR50.a' reading does not capture. Parasitic capacitance alone cannot explain 55 dB.

next_action.task: Examine V2 schematic crop schematics/crops/v2_TL_2x.png for any component (capacitor, resistor, filter network) between the input buffer output (U1B/U2B) and the BLEND pot's dry leg (VR50 pin 1). Also check if the dry tap is taken from a different point than IN_B.
